(born 19 February 1958 in [Deinze](/wiki/Deinze "Deinze")),[[1]](#cite_note-CarBody-1) is a [Belgian](/wiki/Belgians "Belgians") [car designer](/wiki/Car_design "Car design"). He is known for the designing of various models for Volkswagen Group, especially for the [Bentley](/wiki/Bentley "Bentley") brand.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Belgian automobile designer

| Dirk van Braeckel | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | (1958-02-19) 19 February 1958 (age 68) |
| Education | [Royal College of Art](/wiki/Royal_College_of_Art "Royal College of Art") |
| Occupation | Car designer |
| Employer | Audi |

**Dirk van Braeckel** (born 19 February 1958 in [Deinze](/wiki/Deinze "Deinze")),[[1]](#cite_note-CarBody-1) is a [Belgian](/wiki/Belgians "Belgians") [car designer](/wiki/Car_design "Car design"). He is known for the designing of various models for Volkswagen Group, especially for the [Bentley](/wiki/Bentley "Bentley") brand.

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dirk_van_Braeckel&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

Born in [Deinze](/wiki/Deinze "Deinze"),[[1]](#cite_note-CarBody-1) after leaving school, van Braeckel studied electrical engineering,[[2]](#cite_note-ConCar-2) before joining [Ford](/wiki/Ford_Motor_Company "Ford Motor Company") as an [apprentice](/wiki/Apprentice "Apprentice") car designer in [Cologne](/wiki/Cologne "Cologne"), West Germany.[[1]](#cite_note-CarBody-1) Ford then agreed to sponsor him undertake a degree in car design at the [Royal College of Art](/wiki/Royal_College_of_Art "Royal College of Art"), London.[[3]](#cite_note-BusWk-3)

On graduation, he joined [Audi](/wiki/Audi "Audi") in 1984, working on external design[[1]](#cite_note-CarBody-1) including the [Audi A8](/wiki/Audi_A8 "Audi A8") concept and [Audi A3](/wiki/Audi_A3 "Audi A3").[[4]](#cite_note-4) In 1993, [Volkswagen Group](/wiki/Volkswagen_Group "Volkswagen Group") boss [Ferdinand Piëch](/wiki/Ferdinand_Pi%C3%ABch "Ferdinand Piëch") personally chose him to be the design head for Volkswagen's newly purchased [Škoda Auto](/wiki/%C5%A0koda_Auto "Škoda Auto") division, where he revised the entire model line-up,[[3]](#cite_note-BusWk-3) designing the [Octavia](/wiki/%C5%A0koda_Octavia "Škoda Octavia") and [Fabia](/wiki/%C5%A0koda_Fabia "Škoda Fabia") models.[[1]](#cite_note-CarBody-1)

After Volkswagen purchased Bentley in 1998 from [Vickers plc](/wiki/Vickers_plc "Vickers plc"), van Braeckel joined the British-based company in August 1999 as Director of Design and Styling. His brief was to design a car that could sell in higher volumes than the $240,000 [Bentley Arnage](/wiki/Bentley_Arnage "Bentley Arnage") model. Van Braeckel designed the [Bentley Continental GT](/wiki/Bentley_Continental_GT "Bentley Continental GT"), which in 2004 sold 5,983 units, exceeding forecasts by 62%,[[3]](#cite_note-BusWk-3) and in 2009 the [Bentley Mulsanne](/wiki/Bentley_Mulsanne_(2010) "Bentley Mulsanne (2010)") which was designed by him was launched.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Cars

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dirk_van_Braeckel&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cars")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8e/Bentley_Continental_GT-2.jpg/250px-Bentley_Continental_GT-2.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bentley_Continental_GT-2.jpg)

Bentley Continental GT

* [Audi A8](/wiki/Audi_A8 "Audi A8") concept, 1994
* [Audi A3](/wiki/Audi_A3 "Audi A3"), 1996
* [Škoda Octavia](/wiki/%C5%A0koda_Octavia "Škoda Octavia"), 1996
* [Škoda Fabia](/wiki/%C5%A0koda_Fabia "Škoda Fabia"), 1999
* [Škoda Octavia](/wiki/%C5%A0koda_Octavia "Škoda Octavia"), 2004
* [Bentley Continental GT](/wiki/Bentley_Continental_GT "Bentley Continental GT"), 2004
* [Bentley Flying Spur](/wiki/Bentley_Continental_Flying_Spur_(2005) "Bentley Continental Flying Spur (2005)"),[[6]](#cite_note-6) 2005
* [Bentley Azure](/wiki/Bentley_Azure "Bentley Azure"), 2006
* [Bentley Brooklands](/wiki/Bentley_Brooklands "Bentley Brooklands"),[[7]](#cite_note-7) 2007
* [Bentley Mulsanne](/wiki/Bentley_Mulsanne_(2010) "Bentley Mulsanne (2010)"), 2009
* [Bentley EXP 9 F](/wiki/Bentley_EXP_9_F "Bentley EXP 9 F"), 2012[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Awards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dirk_van_Braeckel&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Awards")]

In 2007, van Braeckel was awarded the laureate of [Antwerp](/wiki/Antwerp "Antwerp")'s [Christophe Plantin Prize](/wiki/Christophe_Plantin_Prize "Christophe Plantin Prize"), which honours Belgian citizens whose cultural, artistic or scientific activities contribute to the country's prestige abroad.[[2]](#cite_note-ConCar-2) In 2008, van Braeckel was awarded the European Automotive Design Award by Designers (Europe).[[1]](#cite_note-CarBody-1)
